---
title: "The Literature Zoo"
subtitle: "Cited Scalars for Scaling Laws, MFU, and Communication"
---

The Literature Zoo holds **published anchors** cited in the textbook appendices — MFU bands,
Chinchilla ratios, scaling-efficiency tiers, and communication overheads. Every entry is a
`Sourced` scalar with structured `Provenance` (see [Provenance](../provenance.qmd)).

::: {.callout-important}
## Not hardware specs
Do not confuse `Literature.Training.MfuHigh` with a GPU datasheet field. Literature entries
are cited teaching assumptions; silicon numbers live in [`Hardware`](hardware.qmd).
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import mlsysim
from mlsysim.core.provenance import scalar_value

sections = [
    ("Training MFU bands", mlsysim.Literature.Training),
    ("Scaling efficiency tiers", mlsysim.Literature.Scaling),
    ("Overhead budgets", mlsysim.Literature.Overheads),
    ("Chinchilla anchors", mlsysim.Literature.Chinchilla),
    ("Communication", mlsysim.Literature.Communication),
]

for title, reg in sections:
    print(f"### {title}\n")
    print("| Entry | Value | Description |")
    print("|:---|:---:|:---|")
    for item in reg.list():
        if hasattr(item, "provenance"):
            val = scalar_value(item)
            desc = getattr(item, "description", "") or "---"
            name = getattr(item, "name", type(item).__name__)
        else:
            val = item
            desc = "---"
            name = str(item)
        print(f"| **{name}** | {val} | {desc} |")
    print()
```

## Python Access

```python
import mlsysim

chinchilla_ratio = mlsysim.Literature.Chinchilla.TokensPerParam
mfu_high = mlsysim.Literature.Training.MfuHigh
ring_factor = mlsysim.Literature.Communication.RingAllreduceFactor
```

When calibrating η in [`accuracy.qmd`](../accuracy.qmd), cross-check against these literature
bands before picking solver `efficiency` kwargs.
